title: TOISA 2025: A grand roll call of excellence in Indian sports
content: The Times of India Sports Awards 2025 showcased a grand celebration of Indian sporting excellence, bringing together top athletes, legends, and officials under one roof. The event highlighted outstanding achievements across disciplines, honouring dedication, performance, and resilience. With inspiring moments and recognition of emerging and established stars alike, it reflected India’s growing stature in global sports and its deep talent pool.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX sends email to FCC, says reject Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin application
content: SpaceX has formally responded to Amazon's FCC petition against its satellite data center proposal. SpaceX argues that if Amazon's criticisms are applied, they must also be applied to Blue Origin's similar satellite data center application. SpaceX is pushing for equitable treatment and consistent review of both companies' plans.

title: Why Kerala's 'semi-final' verdict has put Left on red alert ahead of April 9 polls
content: Local body poll results in Kerala signal a potential shift, with the Congress-led UDF gaining momentum against the ruling LDF. The UDF secured key urban strongholds, raising concerns about anti-incumbency for Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan's government. The BJP also shows strategic expansion, hinting at a more competitive assembly election.

title: Iran’s 21-day assault: Full list of US military losses and damage
content: Iran's escalating strikes have inflicted significant operational and reputational costs on the US and Israel, resulting in the loss of over two dozen aircraft and advanced military systems. Relatively low-cost Iranian tactics have successfully targeted expensive US assets, including THAAD radar and an F-35 fighter, raising concerns about modern warfare vulnerabilities.

title: Google announces it is shutting down Firebase Studio, less than a year after its launch
content: Google is shutting down its Firebase Studio AI workspace on March 22, 2027, less than a year after its April 2025 launch. The company is consolidating its AI developer offerings, moving lessons learned into Google AI Studio and Google Antigravity. Core Firebase services remain unaffected, with a year-long transition period for users.

title: Yrs after Zuckerberg called it 'next frontier’ for company, Meta shuts down Horizon worlds
content: Meta is shutting down its virtual reality social network, Horizon Worlds, for Quest headsets by June 15, 2026. The platform, a significant metaverse investment, struggled to gain traction and will transition to a mobile-only experience. This move aims to streamline Meta's VR offerings and focus development efforts.

title: 'I don’t want to force it': Stephen Curry on new step with Ayesha Curry
content: ​Stephen Curry, the Golden State Warriors’ star player and NBA legend, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, seem to have built a pretty strong marriage over the last decade that they have been together as they have become parents to four young children. While the two continue to keep their lives private, Stephen Curry opens up about his hesitation of taking the next step alongside his wife, Ayesha Curry.

title: 'Dhurandhar' day 3: Ranveer Singh film nets Rs 240 cr; eyes to cross Rs 300 cr globally
content: Directed by Aditya Dhar, the high-octane sequel 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is rewriting box office history, netting a staggering Rs. 246.99 crore in just 60 hours. Following the massive success of the 2025 original, the Ranveer Singh-starrer opened to a record-breaking Rs. 102.55 crore on Day 1, maintaining an iron grip on the domestic market despite its sprawling 3-hour and 49-minute runtime. Upped by the festive Ugadi and Gudi Padwa window, the espionage thriller has seen a massive Saturday surge, with Day 3 morning shows alone raking in Rs. 20.72 crore across 6,622 screenings.

title: NHL isider Elliotte Friedman clarifies Toronto Maple Leafs' stance on forward Matthew
content: Contrary to fan speculation, NHL insider Elliotte Friedman clarified that trade talks involving Matthew Knies were not about dissatisfaction with the player. The Maple Leafs were merely assessing his market value, with teams like the Ducks and Devils showing serious interest. Knies remains a highly valued young forward for Toronto.

title: Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding leak sparks Chiefs camp tension
content: A leaked report suggests Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce plan a summer wedding before the NFL season begins, but the timeline raises questions about his training camp availability. Insiders claim the event will be private, indoors, and carefully timed. While close friends and teammates are expected, scheduling conflicts and evolving relationships continue to fuel speculation.

title: Connor Zary injured after dangerous hit by AJ Greer in NHL game
content: The Calgary Flames forward Connor Zary was injured due to a dangerous shot by Florida Panthers AJ Greer in an NHL game. The accident has elicited a lot of discussion about the safety of the players, and observers and consumers have questioned the possible penalty. Zary did not stay until the end of the game, and arguments are being made as to whether an intervention by the NHL Department of Player Safety is necessary or not.

title: Chiefs linked to former Saints $10m pass rusher in NFL rumours
content: According to recent NFL trade rumours, the Kansas City Chiefs are reportedly eyeing veteran edge rusher Cam Jordan to bolster their pass rush. Following a disappointing season,  and the Chiefs' activity in free agency and trades, Jordan's proven production, even at 35, makes him an attractive target. If Kansas City acquires Jordan, it could strengthen its defensive game and have a brighter upcoming season.

title: Afghan player who warned Pak after Kabul attack celebrates with IPL team
content: The celebration took place during a practice session, where players and support staff gathered to mark the occasion.

title: ‘Winter is coming,’ spring is not: Why India’s Rituraj is losing its throne
content: India's cherished spring, once a gentle interlude, is rapidly vanishing.  Winters are warming, heat arrives earlier, and the transition season is shrinking, impacting cultural traditions and agriculture.  Recent years show a disturbing pattern of early heatwaves and record temperatures, signalling a profound shift in the climate. This loss affects not just weather but the very rhythm of life.

title: Ashley Rebello says Karisma tracked Raveena Tandon’s costumes via aide
content: Costume designer Ashley Rebello revealed that the off-screen rivalry between Karisma Kapoor and Raveena Tandon during 'Andaz Apna Apna' extended to their on-screen costumes. Rebello detailed how he enhanced Raveena's nighties for a song, making them more elaborate than Karisma's. He also noted Karisma's team closely monitored Raveena's styling.

title: LeBron James has scary face-off with alligator on golf course—walks away just in time
content: Basketball superstar LeBron James had a close encounter with a sneaky alligator while golfing with his son. Spotting the reptile near a pond where his ball landed, James wisely chose to abandon the ball rather than risk a dangerous confrontation. He demonstrated real-life smarts by prioritizing safety over retrieving his golf ball.

title: In call with PM Modi, Iran prez calls on India-chaired Brics to play role in halting war
content: Iranian President Pezeshkian urged the India-led BRICS to play an independent role in halting aggressions against Iran amid escalating Middle East tensions. He highlighted alleged US and Israeli "unlawful attacks" and crimes, emphasizing Iran's opposition to nuclear weapons. PM Modi condemned attacks on energy infrastructure and called for dialogue and peace.

title: UK says Cyprus bases won't be used for Iran attacks
content: Britain has assured Cyprus its military bases won't be used for offensive action in the Iran conflict, despite bolstering island defenses after a drone incident. Prime Minister Starmer conveyed this commitment, emphasizing Cyprus's security. This comes as the UK allows limited US defensive use of other bases, drawing warnings from Iran.

title: A Rajya Sabha rummy: How BJP manages ‘floating majority’ with bellwether backings
content: ​When the BJP swept to power in 2014 with a decisive majority in the Lok Sabha, it did not carry that dominance into the Rajya Sabha. The Upper House, by design, is insulated from electoral waves. Its members are elected by state assemblies, and their terms are staggered, ensuring continuity and preventing sudden shifts. This meant that even as the BJP commanded brute strength in the Lower House, it remained a minority in the Upper House for years. That imbalance mattered!

title: Ashwin says 'deduct Rs 2 crores' if star Australian fails to bowl 4 overs in IPL
content: During the T20 World Cup, Green scored 24 runs in three innings with a highest score of 21. He bowled three overs across two matches and picked up one wicket. In a recent Sheffield Shield match for Western Australia against New South Wales, where he scored a century, he did not bowl.

title: 'Will put ICE in airports': Trump threatens after bill to fund homeland security failed
content: Former President Donald Trump has threatened to deploy ICE agents to airports for security, demanding Democrats agree to fund airport safety. He specifically targeted immigrants from Somalia, blaming them and Minnesota officials for issues in the state. This comes after a Homeland Security funding bill stalled in the Senate, with Democrats seeking immigration enforcement reforms following recent shootings.

title: 'I did not ask': Gavaskar hits back at the criticism of attending Pakistani Show
content: Sunil Gavaskar has strongly refuted hypocrisy claims regarding his stance on Indian ownership benefiting Pakistani cricketers in overseas leagues. He clarified his commentary roles and denied receiving payment for a Dubai show during the Asia Cup, emphasizing his desire for Indians to cease financially supporting Pakistani players. Gavaskar's remarks follow Abrar Ahmed's signing by Sunrisers Leeds.

title: ‘What are we trying to accomplish?’: Trump faces Congress pressure over Iran war strategy
content: Three weeks into a military campaign against Iran, President Trump faces congressional scrutiny over strategy and funding. Lawmakers demand clear objectives as 13 US service members have been killed. Trump is considering winding down operations, but the War Powers Act necessitates a clear exit strategy or authorization after 45 days.

title: Israeli F-16 hit? IRGC claims jet struck over central Iran; IDF says aircraft not damaged
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed to have downed an Israeli F-16 over central Iran, a charge Israel denies, stating only that one of its aircraft faced missile fire without sustaining damage. This incident follows Iran's earlier assertion of damaging a US F-35. Independent verification of any aircraft being hit remains elusive.

title: Over 20 nations issue joint statement condemning Iran's de facto closure of Hormuz Strait
content: Over 20 nations have pledged support for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, condemning Iran's actions that have effectively closed the vital waterway. Citing attacks on commercial vessels and energy infrastructure, these countries emphasized freedom of navigation as a core international law principle.

title: The door India left ajar: Economic ties with China see a calibrated reset
content: Six years after locking foreign capital out of the door, India has opened it — just wide enough. The Cabinet's amendment to Press Note 3 clears the fog around who can invest from where. What it does not do is throw open the floodgates.

title: ‘Beer, vape, golf clique’: Brendon McCullum faces damning Ashes allegations
content: England's Ashes campaign faced intense scrutiny over dressing-room culture, with allegations of indiscipline and a lack of seriousness. Despite initial setbacks and off-field controversies, Brendon McCullum's job was saved by a decisive shift in approach during the Sri Lanka tour and subsequent World Cup progress.  However, lingering questions remain about the team's ability to perform under pressure.

title: 'Not going to affect us’: India sees no major power supply risk from Middle East crisis
content: India anticipates meeting peak summer electricity demand despite Middle East conflict, relying on coal, renewables, and battery storage. Power Secretary Pankaj Agarwal stated limited gas dependence shields the system. A 4 GW imported coal plant and fast-tracked wind projects will bolster supply, while solar covers daytime needs and batteries address evening demand.

title: ‘B-Team of BJP’: Kerala CM Vijayan slams Rahul Gandhi, Congress ahead of polls
content: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan sharply criticized Congress and Rahul Gandhi, labeling them the "B-team of BJP" and accusing them of failing to learn from past experiences. He highlighted their repeated allegations against Arvind Kejriwal despite court dismissals and claimed Congress aligns with the BJP at both national and state levels.

title: Firing outside Rohit Shetty's house: Mumbai crime branch detains 14th accused in case
content: Mumbai Crime Branch detained the 14th accused, Golu Pandit, in Uttar Pradesh for the firing incident outside Rohit Shetty's home on February 1. The police have invoked stringent MCOCA provisions, suspecting an organized crime syndicate. Investigations are ongoing to retrieve deleted chats and uncover the full conspiracy.

title: Dennis Condrey dead at 74: Dax Harwood shares emotional tribute
content: Dennis “Loverboy” Condrey, legendary Midnight Express wrestler, died at 74. Dax Harwood shared the news and emotional memories, while AEW also paid tribute. Condrey’s work in the 1970s and 1980s helped shape tag team wrestling. Fans and wrestlers continue to honor his legacy, remembering his influence, guidance, and lasting impact on the sport.

title: Thalapathy Vijay visits Mumbai to attend Atlee’s wife baby shower
content: Thalapathy Vijay surprised many with a Mumbai visit for director Atlee's wife Priya's baby shower, showcasing their strong bond beyond cinema. Amidst 'Jana Nayagan' release uncertainties, Vijay's humble airport greeting and attendance at the intimate family function highlight his personal connections. This gesture has resonated deeply with fans, sparking widespread social media celebration.

title: TOISA 2025: India women's cricket teams sweep Team of the Year awards
content: The ceremony in Lucknow recognised athletes and teams across more than 45 sporting categories, honouring performances delivered during the evaluation period from January 1 to December 31, 2025.

title: Fury fizzles: How Trump's Iran ops has spiralled in face of a defiant Tehran - 10 key points
content: Operation Epic Fury, a swift US-Israeli strike targeting Iran's leadership, has unexpectedly morphed into a 22-day attritional war. Despite initial successes, Iran's resilience and regional escalation have caught Washington off guard, turning a planned quick victory into a protracted quagmire with no clear endgame and significant global economic implications.

title: 'CSK don't have 4 IND captains, MI do': Why Pathan picked Mumbai as greatest IPL team
content: Former India pacer Irfan Pathan believes Mumbai Indians edge out Chennai Super Kings as the IPL's greatest team, highlighting MI's significant contribution of match-winners to the Indian squad. Pathan also noted that the upcoming IPL season will be a crucial learning phase for young batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who faces increased scrutiny from opposition bowlers.

title: 'Inflation coming': Rahul's warning over weak rupee; predicts when govt will hike fuel price
content: Rahul Gandhi criticizes the central government over the rupee's record low. He warns of inevitable inflation due to the West Asia crisis impacting fuel prices. Gandhi states production and transport costs will rise, hitting small businesses. He predicts hikes in petrol, diesel, and LPG prices after state elections.

title: 'Wasn't easy': Anaya reveals Sanjay's role after gender-affirming surgery
content: Anaya Bangar shared emotional journey after gender-affirming surgery, saying, "This journey wasn't easy… not just for me, but for my family too." Anaya highlighted Sanjay Bangar’s evolving support, calling it "real, unconditional, and strong." Once a cricketer, Anaya reflected on early identity struggles, recalling, "I used to… say I'm a girl."

title: Sources: UNC, Davis discussing future after loss
content: UNC officials are in discussions with coach Hubert Davis about the coach's future after another first-round loss in the NCAA tournament.

title: Michigan handles Saint Louis to make Sweet 16
content: Yaxel Lendeborg's 25-point outing helped Michigan, the Midwest Region's top seed, power past Saint Louis on Saturday and advance to the Sweet 16.

title: UVA authors first women's upset over No. 7 UGA
content: No. 10 seed Virginia authored the first upset of March Madness this season with an 82-73 win over No. 7 Georgia in Saturday's first round game.

title: Edwards' 27 lead S. Carolina rout of Southern
content: Joyce Edwards had 27 points and eight rebounds to lead South Carolina to a 103-34 rout of Southern in the first round of the women's NCAA tournament.

title: Chelsea routed by Everton to end miserable week
content: A miserable 10 days for Chelsea culminated in a chastening 3-0 defeat as Everton won back-to-back matches at home in the Premier League for the first time this season.

title: Sources: Nuggets' Watson expected to return Sun.
content: Peyton Watson is expected to return for Denver on Sunday against the Trail Blazers after missing more than six weeks due to a hamstring strain.

title: 76ers' Drummond fined $25K for gun-like gesture
content: The NBA has fined Sixers center Andre Drummond $25,000 for making what appeared to be a shooting motion toward the Kings' bench.

title: Mainieri out as South Carolina's baseball coach
content: Paul Mainieri is out as South Carolina's baseball coach midway through his second season after he couldn't approach the level of success he had produced at LSU.

title: 🏀 Ownership subplot affects Giannis' Bucks future
content: As the Giannis trade watch enters another offseason, a rare ownership structure and financial turmoil have left opposing teams wondering: Who's in charge here?

title: Reranking the 32 men's teams left in March Madness
content: And then there were just 32. How do these teams stack up against each other after surviving the first round?

title: UFC London LIVE! Main card underway ahead of Lerone Murphy and Movsar Evloev clash
content: The UFC returns to London with two unbeaten fighters in Lerone Murphy and Movsar Evloev headlining the card and you can follow all the action live right here with ESPN.

title: NHL playoff watch: What's at stake in 11-game Super Saturday?
content: Here's what is on the line in each contest. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings, more.

title: Chase Johnston's first 2-pointer, no subs for Siena and more men's NCAA first-round superlatives
content: What were the coolest, weirdest and most notable things to happen in the men's NCAA tournament's first round? We've got some of the best right here.

title: Women's March Madness Day 1 takeaways: Chalk reigns as better seeds go 16-0
content: We broke down all 16 games, including a triple-double, Tennessee's historic end to its season and a lot of chalk.

title: Why Kenny Pickett was drawn to Panthers by coach Dave Canales
content: Pickett hopes his time in Carolina leads to his "ultimate goal" of getting a starting job in the NFL again.

title: After letting the Super Bowl MVP walk, what's the Seahawks' plan at running back?
content: Kenneth Walker's departure leaves a hole in a backfield already weakened by Zach Charbonnet's injury.

title: Olney: The seven things I took away from MLB spring training
content: What did you miss at major league camps this spring while watching the World Baseball Classic? Here's what stood out to us from Florida and Arizona.

title: Men's tournament: Arizona, Michigan jockey for top odds, St. John's on the move
content: Teams' odds to advance and win the men's college basketball tournament as we move into the round of 32.

title: Women's tournament: UConn the odds-on title favorite heading into Saturday's action
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: Who's your first signing? Most annoying coach? Best city? ESPN's player survey﻿
content: LaLiga players answered ESPN's questions on everything from which one star they would sign for their team to those all-important EA FC player ratings.

title: Potential busts to avoid in 2026
content: Who does Tristan H. Cockcroft think will underachieve in 2026? We have the names to steer clear of when drafting your rosters.

title: Risers and fallers: Doncic stars, but is it time to drop Giannis?
content: Luka Doncic is on another planet, and Ayo Dosunmu and Daniss Jenkins are on the rise, but should fantasy basketball managers keep Giannis Antetokounmpo and Mikal Bridges rostered?

title: Where is the real Liverpool? Maddening inconsistency continues
content: After a dominant midweek showing, Liverpool came crashing back to earth in the Premier League yet again.

title: VAR review: Man United second penalty, Maguire red card
content: Bournemouth got a penalty to snatch a point against Man United, who later had a player sent off. What happened?

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal, Man City, Man United all after Tonali
content: Newcastle midfielder Sandro Tonali is a target for Premier League giants Arsenal, Manchester City and Manchester United. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Kanshi Ram returns
content: The Bahujan Samaj Party founder’s name is being invoked by the leaders in Uttar Pradesh across political lines over the past two weeks and his supporters say that the architect of Dalit consciousness is being resurrected for the State’s Assembly poll next year, finds Mayank Kumar

title: VCK under compulsion to consider local candidates, says Thirumavalavan
content: He also recalls the opinion of the DMK that the party lost in the Arakkonam and Vanur constituencies because its candidates were outsiders

title: T.N. Assembly election: For second time, Pottalurani residents to boycott polls over fish waste processing units
content: The residents have been demanding the closure of fish waste processing units near the village since 2019, and have submitted multiple petitions in this regard. However, the protest intensified in early 2024 ahead of the Lok Sabha election

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: CPI(M) State Committee stands firmon six seats
content: The CPI(M)’s contention is that it had been promised an increase in the seats for the 2021 Tamil Nadu Assembly election by the DMK leadership. On the other hand, the DMK argues that it has included more political parties in the alliance and is likely to lose 15 constituencies from its share.

title: T.N. Assembly election: parties must avoid excessive freebies, focus on development, says ex-Minister Semmalai
content: Contending that these handouts being announced as part of electoral promises by major parties will not facilitate T.N.’s development, he criticises the DMK government for not increasing the capital expenditure substantially over the last 5 years and ‘frittering away’ funds for distributing freebies

title: No blanket exemption for women teachers above 50 from SIR and census: Revenue Department
content: A senior woman teacher at a government school in Bengaluru says that teachers will meet School Education and Literacy Minister Madhu Bangarappa and seek a blanket exemption again.

title: Without nature, human life is unimaginable, says Hemant Soren on Sharhul
content: Jharkhand Chief Minister asserted that had nature not existed, there would be no living beings neither humans nor animals in the world

title: Centre removes caps on airfares after three months
content: The removal of the caps, introduced when widespread cancellation of IndiGo flights triggered a spike in fares, comes at a time when West Asia conflict has led to increase in aviation turbine fuel (ATF)

title: RDG discontinuation fallout: Himachal presents ‘reduced’ budget
content: The Chief Minister started his budget presentation by accusing the BJP for not supporting the State’s interests; Milk procurement price hiked by ₹10

title: BJD suspends six MLAs for defying party’s whip in Rajya Sabha election
content: Regional party’s tally in Odisha Legislative Assembly has come down to 42 from 51 seats won in 2024 assembly election

title: Punjab Minister Bhullar resigns from Cabinet as Opposition demands murder case, CBI probe
content: Laljit Singh Bhullar has been accused of abetting suicide of Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, district manager of the Punjab Warehousing Corporation in Amritsar

title: KLU releases Management Aptitude Test results
content: The university will conduct an entrance examination for engineering courses for three days from March 27

title: Untreated waste, poor civic infra mar Erode (East) Assembly constituency
content: Pollution, traffic congestion, and poor urban infrastructure remain key concerns for voters, who are looking for concrete outcomes

title: South Pars | Same field, two fates
content: An Israeli attack on a gas field straddling the Persian Gulf and Iran’s counterstrikes have laid bare how fragile the world’s energy security really is

title: Religious fervour and prayers for harmony mark Eid-Ul-Fitr
content: Andhra Pradesh Governor S. Abdul Nazeer conveys Eid greetings; Eidgah Committee calls for promotion of humanity over caste and religious differences

title: Supreme Court concerned over cops uploading videos online, says poses threat to fair trial
content: The senior counsel flagged the recent trend of police posting the images of accused persons being handcuffed, tied by ropes, paraded, forced to kneel, etc, on social media, which, apart from causing an affront to personal dignity, adds to the public bias.

title: Process of issuance of PRs to FMGs begins in Andhra Pradesh
content: Around 30 graduates who approached the High Court on the delay have received PRs from March 12 till date; uncertainty continues as the APMC asks for Indian Embassy’s apostille on compensatory certificates

title: IIT Madras launches M.Tech and MA programmes in frontier technologies and governance
content: The programmes have been designed to equip students with interdisciplinary skills and knowledge to address challenges in the fields of governance, industry, and technology

title: SRMIST honours scientists in advanced materials research
content: This was part of the second International Conference on Advanced Functional Materials and Devices organised by its Nanotechnology Research Centre

title: Assembly election highlights: BJP out to claim its share in Bihar: Prashant Kishor on Nitish's exit as CM
content: The Election Commission of India announces the completion of the first randomisation of EVMs and VVPATs in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry

title: Vellore Mayor, councillor booked for violating Model Code of Conduct
content: Inquiry reveals that along with the local ward councillor, Mayor Sujatha Anandakumar allegedly distributed government-sponsored sports equipment to youth in a corporation office at Sathuvachari

title: ‘India’s gem, jewellery sector shows resilience’
content: Mr. Bhansali said reaching ₹2.28 lakh crore in exports with over 4% growth between April 2025 and February 2026, despite a 44% dip in traditional markets like the U.S. was a testament to the industry’s successful strategy of market diversification

title: PM Modi speaks to Iran President; calls for ‘freedom of navigation’ in the Gulf
content: India has so far evacuated around 882 Indian businessmen, students and pilgrims from Iran through neighbouring Armenia and Azerbaijan, MEA Spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said on March 20

title: Kerala polls: 4 LDF Ministers declare assets of ₹2 crore each
content: Ministers K.N. Balagopal, V. Sivankutty, R. Bindu and Roshy Augustine declared assets, both movable and immovable, of over ₹1.94 crore, ₹2.29 crore, ₹2.14 crore and ₹1.84 crore, respectively

title: Government complains about backlog, but feeds it too as the biggest litigant, says Supreme Court judge Nagarathna
content: ‘The state is expected to litigate with restraint and be a model litigator, but that does not happen. It goes on litigating until the end’

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: UDF extends support to four rebel CPI(M) independents; inducts NJD
content: UDF would back G Sudhakaran, who is contesting in Ambalappuzha constituency, P K Sasi in Ottapalam, T K Govindan in Taliparamba, and V Kunhikrishnan in Payyannur

title: Indiaspora to host multiple events in Bengaluru
content: Its 4th Global AI Summit, scheduled for March 25, is expected to examine how AI is rapidly transforming industries and redefining the future of business

title: Politics on Id in West Bengal: Mamata targets Modi; Suvendu pushes Hindutva agenda
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, participating in Id prayers, accuses Prime Minister Narenda Modi of promoting Hindu-Muslim narrative and taking away voting rights. LOP Suvendu Adhikari says he prayed for the well-being of sanatanis at the Kalighat temple.

title: Feminists, lawyers urge MPs to reject ‘unconstitutional’ amendments to transgender Act
content: It narrows the definition of transgender people, takes away the right to self-perceived gender identity, introduces medical gatekeeping, and criminalises transgender people’s support systems, they say in a letter

title: State cannot put ‘arbitrary ceiling’ on disability limits when RPwD Act does not prescribe any: SC
content: Says none of the provisions of the Rights to Persons with Disabilities (RPwD) Act, 2016 indicates that an upper limit could be prescribed in matter of adjudging suitability of a candidate for a particular post.

title: LPG crisis continues to affect hotel industry in Kochi
content: Experts warn that if the situation persists, many more businesses may close, deepening the crisis in the sector

title: Protest in Mathura over death of cow vigilante in road accident; police denies link of smugglers
content: While protesters, mostly cow vigilantes and supporters, clashed with police, alleging that the accident was a deliberate act carried out by cattle smugglers, the police categorically denied such a link to the death.

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: P.K. Sasi to contest as UDF-backed Independent in Ottappalam
content: Congress decides not to allot the expelled CPI(M) leader its ‘hand’ symbol, following objections from a section of local party leaders

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Will the ‘Suresh Gopi effect’ work again in Thrissur?
content: The actor won the 2024 Lok Sabha election from Thrissur with a massive margin of 74,840 votes, giving BJP its first MP from the State. The key question for the party is whether his vote-pulling ability can transfer to another candidate; this time, Padmaja Venugopal in the Assembly election. Critics argue that the sheen of the LS poll victory has since faded

title: Don’t remove any genuine voter from electoral rolls, parliamentary committee tells EC
content: With SIR set to be rolled out in more States, committee seeks a fair and inclusive revision process as well as adequate safeguards for vulnerable sections

title: Sangh backs caste census, not its divisive use: RSS publicity chief Ambekar
content: Ambekar framed the RSS’s approach to caste through social harmony rather than structural confrontation (File photo) Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Adequate coal stocks to meet rising energy needs: Govt
content: Representative image Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. Read More

title: A harrowing time but home at last, recalls crew member of Indian-flagged ship
content: a senior officer of the vessel returned to india early this week after crossing the conflict-hit Strait of Hormuz . the captain and crew were all set to embark on their voyage to India . they were watching news and getting updates of the war and attacks which were happening .

content: umar farooq, the chief cleric of Kashmir, said he was placed under house arrest and the shrine was locked . Omar Abdullah described the situation as 'regrettable' and questioning the administration's claims of "normality"

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: Women's March Madness live updates: Follow all of Saturday's first-round action
content:
The Madness is here! After a thrilling first day, 32 more teams are in action in the 2026 women's NCAA tournament, all airing on ESPN networks. We're tracking all 16 games from Storrs, Connecticut, to Los Angeles. Follow along as our reporters track the live action as the first round comes to a close. Saturday's schedule starts now: ( 9) USC vs. (8) Clemson:  3:30 p.m., ESPN2 
 (15) Fairleigh Dickinson vs. (2) Iowa:  4 p.m., ESPN 
 (13) Miami (OH) vs. (4) West Virginia:  5 p.m., ESPNU 
 (9) Syracuse vs. (8) Iowa State:  5:30 p.m., ESPN2
